body.processed:
A comprehensive health and nutrition tracking platform that helps users monitor their dietary intake, exercise habits, and wellness goals through intuitive visualizations and personalized insights.

The Challenge

NutriTrack needed a web application that could handle complex nutritional data, provide real-time tracking, and deliver a delightful user experience that kept users engaged over the long term. The existing solution was a clunky native app with poor retention rates.

My Approach

I redesigned the entire user experience from the ground up, focusing on progressive disclosure of information and reducing friction in daily logging. The new dashboard uses D3.js for interactive charts and a custom barcode scanning feature for quick food entry.

Results

  • Daily active users increased by 180%
  • Average session duration improved from 2.1 to 7.3 minutes
  • App Store rating improved from 3.2 to 4.7 stars
  • 30-day retention rate jumped from 15% to 42%
body.summary: A comprehensive health and nutrition tracking platform that helps users monitor their dietary intake, exercise habits, and wellness goals through intuitive visualizations and personalized insights.The

projectImage.alt: Health dashboard with charts and nutrition data

body.processed:
A curated marketplace connecting independent artisans with customers who value handmade, sustainable products. The platform supports over 500 sellers and processes thousands of transactions monthly.

The Challenge

CraftMarket's founding team wanted to build a marketplace that felt warm and personal -- the opposite of the cold, algorithmic experience of large e-commerce platforms. They needed a solution that was easy for non-technical sellers to manage while offering sophisticated features like inventory management and shipping integration.

My Approach

I built a custom Next.js storefront with a headless commerce backend using Medusa.js. The seller dashboard was designed to be as simple as possible, with guided setup flows and contextual help. I integrated Stripe Connect for split payments and ShipStation for fulfillment.

Results

  • Launched with 120 sellers, growing to 500+ within 6 months
  • Average order value of $78, well above the initial $50 target
  • 98.5% seller satisfaction rating
  • Featured in TechCrunch as a "marketplace to watch"
body.summary: A curated marketplace connecting independent artisans with customers who value handmade, sustainable products. The platform supports over 500 sellers and processes thousands of transactions monthly.Th

A powerful, self-hosted form builder and survey tool built with accessibility and developer experience as primary design goals. OpenForm supports complex logic, conditional fields, file uploads, and multi-page forms with a drag-and-drop editor.

Motivation

Existing form builders were either expensive SaaS products with vendor lock-in or basic open-source tools that lacked polish. I wanted to create something that offered the UX of Typeform with the flexibility of self-hosting.

Technical Highlights

  • Drag-and-drop form editor built with dnd-kit
  • JSON Schema-based form definitions for portability
  • Webhook integrations with 20+ services
  • WCAG 2.1 AA compliant form rendering
  • Server-side rendering for instant load times

Community

OpenForm has grown to over 3,200 GitHub stars and an active community of contributors. The project has been adopted by several nonprofits and educational institutions as their primary form solution.
